title: Candied Tunisian Rice Pilaf
description: A sweet, pillowy rice pilaf with caramelized shallots and aromatic spices, ideal for a quick weeknight meal.
instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then drain.

In a medium saucepan, combine the rinsed rice, 4 cups of water, a pinch of salt, and the soaked saffron water; bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until the rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
While the rice cooks, heat 1 tsp olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat and add the thinly sliced shallot, cooking until translucent and lightly golden, about 5 minutes.
Stir in 1/4 cup honey and 2 tbsp sugar to the shallots, letting the mixture caramelize and thicken for 2 minutes, then remove from heat.
Once the rice is done, fluff it with a fork and gently fold in the caramelized shallot mixture, ensuring even coating.
Sprinkle 1 tsp ground cumin and 1/2 tsp ground coriander over the rice, mixing thoroughly to distribute the spices.
Gently stir in 1 tbsp chopped fresh mint, adding a fresh brightness to the pilaf.
Transfer the pilaf to a korean-brass-bowl, optionally garnish with 1 tsp lemon zest for a citrus aroma.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
